treatment_information.specific_treatment: Ablation at the site of the slow pathway potential
treatment_information.treatment_response: The AVNRT attack did not recur even when performing the back somersault.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient experienced palpitations after landing a round-off back somersault.
Electrophysiologic study (EPS) revealed an intra-nodal dual pathway causing AVNRT.
Radiofrequency catheter ablation was applied at the site of the slow pathway potential.
After the procedure, the tachycardia attack did not recur even when performing the back somersault.

patient_outcome.final_status: Healthy and active gymnast with no symptoms
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up for the tiny ASD, no significant changes in hemodynamics and echocardiographic findings observed for several years.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Healthy and active gymnast with no symptoms
